document.docTitle: Cylindroeme yunnanensis Lin & Li 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: A new species, Cylindroeme yunnanensis sp. nov. from China (Coleoptera, Cerambycidae, Cerambycinae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A new species, Cylindroeme yunnanensis sp. nov. from China (Coleoptera, Cerambycidae, Cerambycinae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Engineering Research Center for Forest and Grassland Disaster Prevention and Reduction, Mianyang Normal University, 166 Mianxing West Road, Mianyang, Sichuan, 621000, China.

Kunming Natural History Museum of Zoology, Kunming Institute of Zoology, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Kunming 650223, China. likaiqin @ mail. kiz. ac. cn; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0001 - 7854 - 8445

heading.emphasis:
Cylindroeme yunnanensis
sp. nov. 云fflåfihk牛

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 1–2.
Cylindroeme yunnanensis
sp. nov .
1. Holotype, male, from Yunnan; 2. Paratype, female, from Yunnan; a. dorsal view; b. lateral view; c. ventral view; d. Head, pronotum and scutellum, dorsal view; e. Head in frontal view. Scale bar = 2 mm. 1e & 2e, scale bar = 0.5mm = 500 um, 1d & 2d, not to scale.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Type specimens examined.
Holotype : male ( Fig. 1 ), China , Yunnan , Kunming ( Ễfi ),
1984.V.16
, Da-Rong Yang leg. ( KIZ )
. Paratypes : 1 female ( Fig. 2 ), the same as holotype data;
13 males and 10 females , China , Yunnan , Kunming (
Ễfi
),
1984.V.16
, Da-Rong Yang leg. ( KIZ )
;
1 male and 1 female , China , Yunnan , Kunming , alt.
2200m
, host:
Pyrus pashia Buch.
- Ham. ex D.Don ,
1984.V.16
, Da-Rong Yang leg. ( KIZ )
;
7 males and 8 females , China , Yunnan , Kunming (
Ễfi
), Huahongdong ( ĖË洞 ), alt.
2200m
, host:
Pyrus pashia Buch.
- Ham. ex D.Don ,
1984. V.16
, Da-Rong Yang leg. ( KIZ )
;
1 male , China , Yunnan , Kunming (
Ễfi
), Huahongdong ( ĖË洞 ), alt.
2200m
, host:
Cupressus funebris Endl., 1984
.VIII.8, Da-Rong Yang leg. ( KIZ )
.

Female. Body length 8.3–12.7 mm , humeral width 1.3–2.0 mm. Similar to male, but antennae shorter than body ( Fig. 2b ); pronotum nearly as long as broad, with discal ridges less complete and less obvious than in the male ( Fig. 2d ); elytra about 4.5 times as long as humeral width.

Remarks. This new species is most similar to the type species,
C. vietnamica ,
but it can be separated by elytron without a longitudinal basal ridge ( Figs. 1a, 1d, 2a vs. 8a, 8d); male with median notch at apex of pronotum less remarkable, and farther from pronotal apical margin, ridge on pronotum more distinct and complete ( Figs. 1d , 11 vs. 8d, 10); female scutellum slender ( Fig. 2d ). It can easily be distinguished from
C. shii
by the upper eye lobes closer to each other, almost connected ( Figs. 1d, 2d , 11 ).

Comparing the male genitalia with
C. vietnamica
, the new species has sternite VIII less protruding angles at sides ( Fig. 3c vs. Fig. 13c ), tegmen with lateral lobes even shorter ( Fig. 6 vs. Fig. 16 ), apex of ventral plate of median lobe sharper ( Fig. 7 vs. Fig. 17 ), the ratio of medion struts / median lobe lower (ca. 3/5 vs. ca. 3/4).
Etymology. The new species is named after the type locality, Yunnan Province , China .
